titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Sweden ( (Johnson))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Sweden ( Johnson )
div.p: 749. To Johnson and Olsen from War Refugee Board. This is WRB’s cable no. 4. Reference your 1235 of April 11.Not printed. The Board appreciates importance of Hellsted’sOfficial of Swedish Foreign Office in charge of foreign interests. statement regarding extent of danger to many Germans, Swedes and others from Gestapo in Finland. Regardless of religion, nationality or stateless status Board is charged with rescuing “Victims in imminent danger of death from enemy oppression”. Therefore Swedish action to afford rescue to all groups in Finland especially in danger by increased German influence would be warmly welcomed by Board.

Board suggests that with respect to Hellsted’s remark that permission to enter Sweden to refugees and others would discredit Finnish Government, you bring to his attention Boheman’sErik C. Boheman, Swedish Under Secretary of State. statement to the effect that the Finnish Government has applied itself for the admission of Jewish refugees as reported in your 908.See footnote 4, p. 1036.

Answering questions final paragraph your 1235, Board holds that all persons described in Department’s 724, April 21 are in danger.
Board is prepared to make arrangements for the evacuation of all persons other than Swedes who may be accorded refuge in Sweden as soon as practicable from Sweden and for the support of such refugees who cannot claim the support of their own governments in Sweden. Swedish action (reported in your 1379 of April 21Not printed.) in authorizing 100 visas to central European refugees now in Finland as a starter is appreciated. Danger threatening not only the 113 Jewish refugees from Austria and Germany but also the 2600 others described in Department’s 1724 [724] of April [21] is a matter of deep concern to Board. Your representations as reported in your 1209 of 8thNot printed. have full support of Board.

Board assumes, in light of your 1379 of April 21, that the Hellsted statement does not modify in any way the confidential assurances reported in your 908 of March 16 as given by Boheman. The Board will welcome any confirmation which you may be able to secure of these assurances and if at any time you consider that danger is imminent the Board requests that you press immediately for appropriate action.The Swedish Foreign Office later agreed to issue visas to some 60 central European Jews remaining in Finland, for the most part unemployables of German, Polish, Lithuanian, and Rumanian origin, on condition that assurances be given as to maintenance of this group in Sweden. The necessary assurances were promptly given by the United States Government and arrangements were made for evacuation of this group to Sweden.
Board is gratified by favorable Swedish response to Olsen’s appointment (reference Olsen’s no. 3Telegram 1126, April 3, not printed.) and approves statements made to press. [War Refugee Board.]
